content.content:
- content: "Acme Analytics uses managed external guest access for Slack workspaces.
\
    Before an invitation or invitation request is created, the requester must
consider\
    \ the user's Slack role, workspace plan and invitation setting,
target domain\
    \ classification, target channel classification, and current
request record.
\
    
Workspace control records expose configuration rather than user-facing
Slack\
    \ procedures:

- EXT-PARTNER-DIRECT-04 is associated with an approved_partner\
    \ domain and a
  standard_collaboration_channel. Its Slack invitation permission\
    \ setting
  is standard_workspace_invitation.

- EXT-GUEST-REVIEW-17 is associated\
    \ with a not_listed domain and a
  restricted_project_channel. Its Slack invitation\
    \ permission setting is
  require_admin_approval, and its configured request\
    \ route is the
  designated guest approver queue.

- A control record classified\
    \ as blocked has no configured invitation or
  invitation-request action surface.
\
    
The control record does not select the user-facing Slack action. Use
Slack's\
    \ official invitation guidance to interpret the current role, plan,
and invitation\
    \ permission setting and determine whether the supported
action is a direct guest\
    \ invitation or an invitation request.

The Acme Analytics workspace exposes\
    \ both configured action surfaces:

Direct guest invitation:
https://app.slack.com/admin/W-ATLAS-2048/invitations/new
\
    
Invitation request:
https://app.slack.com/admin/W-ATLAS-2048/invitations/requests
\
    
Use only the action surface supported by Slack's official procedure for
the\
    \ current role and invitation permission setting.

Before creating either action,\
    \ inspect the current guest-invite process
record and avoid creating a duplicate\
    \ action for the same external person
and channel scope.

Both workspace action\
    \ surfaces accept the target guest email, requested
guest role, target channel\
    \ scope, workspace identifier, and business
purpose. Equivalent field labels\
    \ may be used by the submission client.
"
  metadata: {}
  rank: 1
  title: Acme Analytics Slack External Guest Access Policy
  url: null

args.query: site:slack.com/help invite guests Slack admin approval single-channel guest workspace member

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Require admin approval for invitations to Slack | Slack

  - url: https://slack.com/help/articles/115004854783-Require-admin-approval-for-invitations-to-Slack

  - source: Slack Help Center

  - domain: slack.com


  ## Content


  # Require admin approval for invitations to Slack


  By default, members, but not guests, can invite new members to a Slack workspace.
  Workspace Owners and Workspace Admins can change the invitation permission so that
  invitations must be requested and approved. When an invitation request is approved
  or denied, Slack notifies the member who submitted the request.


  ## Manage invitation permissions


  Workspace Owners and Workspace Admins can manage whether workspace invitations require
  approval:


  1. From the desktop app, click **Admin** in the sidebar.

  2. Select **Workspace settings**.

  3. Open the **Permissions** tab.

  4. Next to **Invitations**, click **Expand**.

  5. Check or clear **Require admin approval**.

  6. Save the setting.


  Slack notes that only owners and admins can manage guest accounts. Owners and admins
  approve members'' requests to invite guests.


  ## Manage where invitation requests are sent


  When admin approval is required, the workspace can choose where invitation requests
  are delivered. Requests may be sent to all admins through Slack notifications or
  routed to a specific Slack channel.


  To configure request routing, an owner or admin opens Workspace settings, selects
  the Permissions tab, expands Invitations, confirms that admin approval is required,
  and chooses whether requests should go to all admins or to a selected channel.


  ## Enterprise invitation-request management


  On Enterprise plans, Org Owners and Org Admins can use Slack APIs to build a custom
  invitation-request management app. Such an app may connect Slack invitation requests
  to an identity provider or IT ticketing system. When an organization installs a
  custom invitation-request management app, invitation requests are routed to Org
  Owners and Org Admins rather than Workspace Owners and Workspace Admins.


  This feature is available to Workspace Owners and Workspace Admins on all Slack
  plans. The article describes the official approval model and request-routing behavior;
  the workspace''s own access policy determines whether a particular target and scope
  are subject to that model.

  '

content.content:
I submitted an admin-approval request for:

- **Nora Lee** — nora.lee@consultantco.io  
- **Access:** Single-channel guest  
- **Channel:** #proj-atlas-review  
- **Purpose:** Review Project Atlas analytics deliverables with the internal project team

A direct invitation isn’t available because the consultant’s domain isn’t allowlisted and the channel is restricted. Slack’s configured workflow requires admin approval.

The submission endpoint accepted the request, though the workspace status check hasn’t yet reflected a pending request. An owner or admin will need to approve it before Nora can access the channel.
